feature_name: Dual operation modes: Root Complex and End Point
description: Module can operate as either a Root Complex (RC) or an End Point (EP). Bootstrap pins PCIESSMODE[1:0] select default mode and software can override via DEVSTAT. RC mode provides configuration access to downstream devices, while EP mode exposes PCIe Type 0 space. Functional behavior and accessible registers vary by mode. This flexibility enables a single silicon to serve host or device roles.
dependencies: PCIESSMODE[1:0] straps; DEVSTAT register; Link training enabled via CMD_STATUS[LTSSM_EN]
Remarks: Ensure correct mode before link training and configuration; reinit when changing modes.

feature_name: Link width x1/x2 with Gen1/Gen2 speeds
description: Supports a single bidirectional link up to two lanes wide. Operates at 2.5 GT/s (Gen1) and 5.0 GT/s (Gen2) per lane with automatic width conversion in PHY. Automatic lane reversal and polarity inversion on RX are supported. Dynamic-width conversion allows operation across different partner configurations. Ultra-low TX/RX latency is targeted.
dependencies: Common clock considerations per Section 2.2; SerDes PLL lock
Remarks: Use LINK_CTRL2[TGT_SPEED] and PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD] to move to Gen2 after Gen1 link-up.

feature_name: Supported PCIe transactions (posted/non-posted, cfg, msg)
description: Implements most PCIe transaction types. Supports memory read/write, configuration Type 0/1 reads and writes, and message requests with/without data. I/O transactions inbound are not supported. Locked memory read and corresponding completion locked are not supported.
dependencies: Operating mode dependent routing (RC vs EP)
Remarks: Avoid unsupported inbound I/O and locked reads to prevent UR/completer abort.

feature_name: Address Space 0 windows (local/remote cfg, remote I/O, app regs)
description: Provides a 16 KB Address Space 0 subdivided into four 4 KB regions. Regions map to application registers, local configuration space, remote configuration access window, and RC remote I/O window. Accesses must be single 32-bit and non-burst. Software must avoid remote windows when link is down.
dependencies: Operational link; IOBASE setup for RC
Remarks: Mark regions as non-cacheable; poll link before remote accesses.

feature_name: Outbound address translation (32 regions, 1–8MB)
description: Outbound mapper splits device memory into 32 equal regions (1/2/4/8 MB). Each region remaps to PCIe address via OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI when OB_XLT_EN is set. Used for memory TLPs only with 32/64-bit addressing support. Transactions must not cross region boundaries.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS[OB_XLT_EN]=1; OB_SIZE programmed; EDMA or CPU master
Remarks: Choose region size to avoid boundary crossings; program high dword for 64-bit targets.

feature_name: Inbound address translation (4 regions) with BAR match
description: Inbound mapper provides four regions keyed by BAR hits. Each region uses IB_BARn, IB_STARTn_LO/HI and IB_OFFSETn to translate PCIe addresses into internal bus addresses. BAR0 is reserved for application registers and cannot be remapped. 64-bit pairs (BAR[2-3],[4-5]) supported in EP mode.
dependencies: Proper BAR programming; CMD_STATUS[IB_XLT_EN]=1
Remarks: For BAR1 special mode, leave IB_START1_LO/HI zero to use BAR1 as start; restrict mappings to Address Space 1.

feature_name: BAR Mask overlay configuration (DBI_CS2)
description: Allows software to preset BAR sizes/types before RC enumeration. BAR Mask registers are writable only when CMD_STATUS[DBI_CS2]=1 and overlay BAR offsets. Read back must confirm write completion due to posted behavior. BAR0 mask limited to bits [31:20]; BAR0 fixed to app regs.
dependencies: Enable DBI_CS2; device internal bus access
Remarks: Clear DBI_CS2 before enumeration; verify masks by pattern write-read to BARs.

feature_name: Endian conversion for inbound/outbound accesses
description: Subsystem supports big and little endian despite PCIe core being little-endian at pins. ENDIAN[1:0] selects byte swap granularity for inbound/outbound paths. Tables define big-endian swizzle modes for 1/2/4/8-byte lanes. For MMR/non-prefetchable uses 32-bit swap (Mode C).
dependencies: System endianness boot config; ENDIAN register setup
Remarks: Ensure data structures use matching byte enables; validate FBE/LBE with chosen mode.

feature_name: DMA support via internal master/slave ports
description: No integrated DMA engine, but supports DMA through chip interconnect. Slave port services outbound EDMA writes/reads; master port sources inbound TLPs to memory. Bandwidth targets ~85% of 2.0/4.0 Gbps per lane accounting for 8b/10b and overhead.
dependencies: External EDMA; address translation configured; bus mastering enabled
Remarks: Align bursts to 128B, limit misaligned outbound writes to 120B per requirements.

feature_name: Transaction and burst alignment constraints
description: Internal bus limits transactions to 128 bytes; misaligned outbound writes must be <=120 bytes. Only incremental burst type is supported; fixed/wrap cause undefined behavior. Byte enables must be continuous 1s without holes. Read responses are not interleaved if <= configured max size (128B).
dependencies: Core configuration for max transaction size; EDMA settings
Remarks: Structure buffers and strides to 128B boundaries; enforce continuous byte strobes on writes.

feature_name: Interrupt system: legacy and MSI (generation/reception)
description: Provides 14 interrupt events including legacy INTA-D (RC reception and EP generation) and 32-vector MSI support in both RC and EP. MSI generation uses EP memory write to RC MSI_IRQ with vector in data. Status, enable/clear registers per MSI group; IRQ_EOI used to finalize events.
dependencies: MSI_CAP/MSI_* configured; BAR0 reachability; interrupt controller mapping
Remarks: Clear MSIn_IRQ_STATUS and write IRQ_EOI after handling; disable relaxed ordering for posted write/interrupt ordering if needed.

feature_name: Advanced Error Reporting (AER) and baseline error handling
description: Implements PCIe baseline error reporting via DEV_STAT_CTRL and Status/Command registers and full AER extended capability. Correctable and uncorrectable error status/mask/severity and header log are provided. Root Complex can gate SERR and PME behaviors via ROOT_CTRL_CAP and ROOT_ERR_CMD.
dependencies: AER enabled in DEV_STAT_CTRL and ROOT_ERR_CMD; masks configured
Remarks: Tune mask/severity to application tolerance; disable ECRC if peer lacks support (PCIE_ACCR note).

feature_name: ECRC generation/checking controls
description: Supports optional End-to-End CRC generation and checking. PCIE_ACCR exposes ECRC_GEN_EN/CHK_EN and capability bits to match partner support. First Error Pointer aids root-cause by indicating earliest uncorrectable set bit.
dependencies: Peer capability; driver policy
Remarks: Disable ECRC when peers do not support (see note in 2.16.2.3.1) to avoid false errors.

feature_name: Power management: ASPM L0s/L1 and device D-states
description: Hardware supports Active State Power Management for L0s/L1 and device power states D0–D3. Software can force link L1 via PM_CTL_STAT and configure ASPM in LINK_CAP/STAT. Relationship between device/link states is defined to maintain coherency. PME messaging and PM interrupts are supported.
dependencies: Platform clocking; ASPM policy; ROOT/EP roles
Remarks: Coordinate with OS PCIe PM; set CLK_PWR_MGMT and ASPM bits per latency budget.

feature_name: PIPE and PHY loopback modes
description: Supports PIPE loopback (master/slave roles) for symbol-level testing and PHY loopback for TLP-level when in RC. PHY loopback requires SerDes configuration (RX/TX loopback enable) and LTSSM force to POLL_ACTIVE, with constraints on BAR mapping and base/limit to avoid misrouting.
dependencies: RC mode for PHY loopback; SerDes configured and PLL locked
Remarks: Set loopback before any traffic to avoid sequence number mismatch; skip config transactions in PHY loopback.

feature_name: Software and hardware reset mechanisms
description: Supports power-on reset (cold), system reset (hard/soft), and software-initiated downstream hot reset. RSTCMD[INIT_RST] triggers hot reset; FLUSH_N indicates outstanding transactions. Post reset, software must wait minimum timing before re-accessing PCIe.
dependencies: PSC/domain control; link quiescence
Remarks: Observe 100 ms min after reset; ensure traffic is stopped before power domain cycling.

feature_name: SerDes PLL and reference clock configuration
description: KeyStone I SerDes exposes CFGPLL and STS registers to select PLL multiply and loop bandwidth, enable PLL, and monitor LOCK. Reference clock typically 100 MHz; choose MPY to target 2.5 GHz line rate for both Gen1/Gen2. PLL must lock before any PCIESS register access is reliable.
dependencies: Quality reference clock; Kicker unlock for chip config; PSC
Remarks: Allow ~1 µs regulator settle and verify STS[LOCK]=1 before enabling LTSSM.

feature_name: Port/link controls and training parameters
description: Port Logic registers allow forcing link states, enabling DLL, selecting de-emphasis and margins, and setting ACK frequency and training sequences. LINK_CTRL2 controls target speed and compliance behaviors. These aid bring-up, compliance, and tuning for jitter/EMI.
dependencies: Proper sequencing with LTSSM; partner capabilities
Remarks: Use PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD] post L0 to switch to Gen2; set ACK_FREQ>0 only if required by validation.

feature_name: Transaction filtering and policy controls
description: Filter mask registers allow dropping or relaxing checks for CFG/IO/MSG TLPs, ECRC, attribute/length/TC/function matching, UR treatments, and vendor messages. Useful for testing and special routing cases in RC. Defaults enforce strict compliance.
dependencies: Careful policy configuration; RC vs EP behavior
Remarks: Use only in controlled environments; improper masking may hide protocol violations.

feature_name: MSI and legacy interrupt register interface (BAR0 mapping)
description: Application registers including MSI_IRQ and EP/legacy IRQ sets are mapped to BAR0 for EP/RC access. RC can generate MSI to EP by writing EP’s BAR0 MSI_IRQ. Grouped enable/status/clear registers manage 32-vector space in banks of 4.
dependencies: BAR0 accessibility from peer; capability bits set
Remarks: Ensure INTx disabled when MSI enabled (MSI_CAP[MSI_EN]=1); write-one-to-clear semantics.

feature_name: Debug: LTSSM state visibility and PIPE signals
description: DEBUG0/DEBUG1 registers expose LTSSM state encoding, received idle, TS detection, lane reversal, and transmit indicators. Appendix A maps encoded values (e.g., L0=0x11) to human-readable states. Useful for link bring-up diagnostics and error triage.
dependencies: None
Remarks: Poll during training to locate stalls; correlate with LINK_STAT_CTRL[DLL_ACTIVE].

feature_name: Initialization sequences for RC and EP
description: Prescribed steps include powering/clocks, setting mode, programming SerDes, waiting PLL lock, disabling LTSSM, programming PCIESS registers, enabling LTSSM, validating LTSSM state L0, and continuing enumeration/handshake. EP may enable APP_RETRY_EN during slow init.
dependencies: PSC; SerDes; LTSSM
Remarks: Follow order strictly; clear APP_RETRY_EN once ready to accept config accesses.

feature_name: Byte enable and payload limits enforcement
description: Writes require contiguous byte enables within a TLP; holes are not allowed. Max payload sizes are 128 B outbound, 256 B inbound; max remote read request size is 256 B. Violations can lead to undefined behavior or bus lock. Configure DEV_STAT_CTRL for payload and request sizes.
dependencies: EDMA/driver conformance
Remarks: Set MAX_PAYLD and MAX_REQ_SZ per platform; validate driver pack/unpack of strobes.

feature_name: MSI/Legacy and Error/PM consolidated interrupt outputs (14 lines)
description: PCIESS exposes up to 14 interrupt lines to the SoC interrupt controller, aggregating MSI banks, legacy, error, and power/reset events. Software writes IRQ_EOI with event number to complete handling. Error events include advanced error, AXI fatal, and system error OR.
evidence: Table 2-10; IRQ_EOI (Sec. 3.1.15); ERR_* and PMRST_* regs (Sec. 3.1.72–3.1.79)
dependencies: Interrupt controller mapping; enable bits set
Remarks: Map each event to ISR with write-one-to-clear and EOI sequences to avoid retrigger.

feature_name: Hot-plug/slot capability fields for RC
description: RC exposes Slot Capability and Slot Status/Control fields to represent presence, indicators, interlock, and power control. While no built-in hot-plug hardware exists, these registers allow system software representation and control per PCIe spec. Command Complete and HP interrupts are supported fields.
dependencies: Platform implementation of slot power/indicators
Remarks: Use only if platform wiring exists; otherwise leave capabilities cleared to avoid OS enabling hot-plug.

feature_name: Big-endian byte strobe tables and requirements
description: Provides explicit byte-lane swizzle tables for inbound/outbound in big-endian systems. This ensures correct FBE/LBE pairing across 64-bit internal bus while PCIe counts in dwords. Driver must program ENDIAN mode consistent with CPU endianness and buffer alignment.
dependencies: CPU endianness; buffer layout
Remarks: Test with mixed-size transfers (1/2/4/8B) to verify correctness under selected swizzle mode.
